title: MANGA NGE Volume 10s release
wikitext:
[]: [NGE] Volume 10's release             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Thu Aug 24, 2006 10:12 am According to amazon, it's going to be released in 10 April 2007!

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Thu Aug 24, 2006 12:36 pm Good to know there isn't a very long wait then.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Aug 24, 2006 12:57 pm Before anyone gets ahead of themselves, Amazon appear to be the only one even listing it at this point. April 10th of next year seems like an awfully large over-estimation to me. The Japanese edition came out at the end of March, and translation work began in April at Viz. Carl Horn told me just a few weeks ago that November was his best estimate. And given that it typically takes 6 months start to finish on a single volume like that, November seems far more reasonable than next April. So, I'm calling schenanigans on Amazon.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Aug 24, 2006 1:28 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Before anyone gets ahead of themselves, Amazon appear to be the only one even listing it at this point. April 10th of next year seems like an awfully large over-estimation to me. The Japanese edition came out at the end of March, and translation work began in April at Viz. Carl Horn told me just a few weeks ago that November was his best estimate. And given that it typically takes 6 months start to finish on a single volume like that, November seems far more reasonable than next April. So, I'm calling schenanigans on Amazon.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Tue Aug 29, 2006 12:07 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Before anyone gets ahead of themselves, Amazon appear to be the only one even listing it at this point. April 10th of next year seems like an awfully large over-estimation to me. So, I'm calling schenanigans on Amazon.

[]:             Quote Post by WES512 » Mon Oct 02, 2006 9:16 am Here in Argentina, each volume is released as two parts. The publisher is Ivrea. So for volume 10 I will get volume 19 and 20 separately. The release date for Vol. 19 is stated for October 5th. And on December 2006 Vol. 20 will hopefully get released as well. [url]http://www.editorialivrea.com/ARG/imagenes/noticias/evangelion_19.jpg[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 02, 2006 11:32 am WES512 wrote: Here in Argentina, each volume is released as two parts. The publisher is Ivrea. So for volume 10 I will get volume 19 and 20 separately. The release date for Vol. 19 is stated for October 5th. And on December 2006 Vol. 20 will hopefully get released as well. [url]http://www.editorialivrea.com/ARG/imagenes/noticias/evangelion_19.jpg[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by WES512 » Mon Oct 02, 2006 4:35 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: WES512 wrote: Here in Argentina, each volume is released as two parts. The publisher is Ivrea. So for volume 10 I will get volume 19 and 20 separately. The release date for Vol. 19 is stated for October 5th. And on December 2006 Vol. 20 will hopefully get released as well. [url]http://www.editorialivrea.com/ARG/imagenes/noticias/evangelion_19.jpg[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Oct 03, 2006 6:36 pm I hope Viz markets Vol. 10 properly. Remember how it seemed to be almost impossible to get ahold of a copy of Vol. 9?

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Tue Oct 03, 2006 10:46 pm Really? I've seen it in every comic book store I've bothered to look for it in, and Amazon has it in stock.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Oct 04, 2006 1:15 pm cat42 wrote: Really? I've seen it in every comic book store I've bothered to look for it in, and Amazon has it in stock.

[]:             Quote Post by MrPhil » Sun Nov 12, 2006 11:57 pm I'll probably be waiting even longer for Vol. 10. First Singaporean publisher has to Chuang Yi release it (they use Viz's scripts by the looks of it), then it'll have to be released in Australasia (The Chuang Yi's mangas are distributed here by Madman). All this for consistency in releases, I normally don't give a stuff regarding DVDs; but oddly do for these manga.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Mon Dec 18, 2006 10:10 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Before anyone gets ahead of themselves, Amazon appear to be the only one even listing it at this point. April 10th of next year seems like an awfully large over-estimation to me. The Japanese edition came out at the end of March, and translation work began in April at Viz. Carl Horn told me just a few weeks ago that November was his best estimate. And given that it typically takes 6 months start to finish on a single volume like that, November seems far more reasonable than next April. So, I'm calling schenanigans on Amazon.

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Thu Jan 04, 2007 2:06 am http://www.viz.com/products/products.php?product_id=6492 viz says April 10, so it MUST be true...

[]:             Quote Post by Big Panda » Thu Jan 04, 2007 2:14 am WES512 wrote: Here in Argentina, each volume is released as two parts. The publisher is Ivrea. So for volume 10 I will get volume 19 and 20 separately. The release date for Vol. 19 is stated for October 5th. And on December 2006 Vol. 20 will hopefully get released as well. [url]http://www.editorialivrea.com/ARG/imagenes/noticias/evangelion_19.jpg[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 04, 2007 2:29 am I would wager money that Sadamoto will manage to put out another stage in one of the next few issues of Shonen Ace, and then they'll manage to get the Japanese volume 11 on stands before the english volume 10 gets published by Viz. And then we'll get to wait for TWO volumes.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Thu Jan 04, 2007 2:35 pm I would wager money that Sadamoto won't release another stage at least until after movie 1 of rebuild is out.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Thu Jan 04, 2007 4:50 pm What is the wager that he will take another year or two hiatus from the project entirely?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 04, 2007 5:24 pm Oh come on, let's not be so pessimistic. He's been pretty good lately. Dec - 70 Apr - 71 Jun - 72 Aug - 73 Oct - 74 Nov - 75 Dec - 76 Before stage 71, Sadamoto was doing one stage every three months. Since stage 71, he's been doing stages pretty consistantly. He skipped a few months, but he's making significant progress now, so I'm thinking he might just stick to it and finish the comic up in the next two years. If he sticks to 13 volumes, he's got 15 months minimum.

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Fri Jan 05, 2007 1:37 am The Eva Monkey wrote: I would wager money that Sadamoto will manage to put out another stage in one of the next few issues of Shonen Ace, and then they'll manage to get the Japanese volume 11 on stands before the english volume 10 gets published by Viz. And then we'll get to wait for TWO volumes.
summary:
[]: [NGE] Volume 10's release             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Thu Aug 24, 2006 10:12 am According to amazon, it's going to be released in 10 April 2007!

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Thu Aug 24, 2006 12:36 pm Good to know there isn't a very long wait then.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Mo
